author: @neatt3815
text: I like that there's no need for soldering 👍

replies.text: I do as well, I'm awful at soldering, This makes it super easy. :)

author: @robertpitt8418
text: Looking good luke , cant wait to see those joysticks and buttons in place

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: Thanks mate! They fit really nicely. :)

author: @MrZacchery
text: Thank you, I have been comparing buttons, what brand are these? My fightstick uses Qanba, some use Seimitsu.

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: The brand of these specific ones is Reyann, They were really nicely priced aswell. Thanks mate! :)

author: @kevinleebailey
text: MAME arcade games would be great to run on this machine once it's finished an old clapped out PC should be good to run the emulator ! Great stuff !

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: I agree mate MAME is fantastic! II was going to use a Raspberry Pi, now I'm wondering if a clapped-out computer would be better to use. Thanks for the suggestion mate! :)

author: @TheMan1510
text: I'm guessing those smaller buttons might be used to simulate coins being dropped into the machine.

replies.author: @kevinleebailey
replies.text: I would say they are for one or two player mode.

author: @pulesjet
text: Are the joy sticks switching type or pot type/

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: To be honest, I'm not too sure, the brand that I'm using is reyann. Hope this helps mate! :)

author: @rastamanralph6670
text: I missed the last video but that looks like a canny bit of kit like👍 I collect consoles and have loads of retro console's and games. I've also got a mini snes with 261 games on and that would be great with your cabinet. I got it cheap from eBay. Have you got a machine to use in it yet?  The games are from loads of consoles like nes,,megadrive and others with your favourite games like loads of Mario's and Sonics and the such.

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: That's awesome! I used to have a Gameboy thing that was loaded with games which sounds kinda similar it was super! With this build, I'm going to be using a Raspberry Pi with RetroPi. This firmware is fantastic, and can be loaded with pretty much anything. Thanks Mate! :)

author: @stevetobias4890
text: Why did I know that you would head down the raspberry pi road to do the game encoding. I guess it's the popular choice these days with the coding preconfigured for many gaming platforms.

replies.author: @NorthernKitty
replies.text:
RetroPie just makes it too easy not to just grab a Raspberry Pi and be off to the arcade!!!  😊
But you could also use a PC or an ODroid C1/C2 with RetroPie.  I think if I built a unit similar to Luke's, I'd use an inexpensive single-board PC with a decent video processor so that it could double as a multi-media station.  You could of course use a Pi for multimedia, too, but the video is rather underpowered and can get a bit choppy.

replies.author: @stevetobias4890
replies.text: @NorthernKitty also with the pi zero it makes it easy to retrofit a Gameboy console
